tasks.title: Add reporting and analytics dependencies
tasks.description:
Install required dependencies for report generation, export, and analytics.

**Dependencies to add:**
1. `npm install recharts` - for data visualization and charts
2. `npm install xlsx` - for Excel export functionality
3. `npm install jspdf jspdf-autotable` - for PDF generation
4. `npm install pptxgenjs` - for PowerPoint export
5. `npm install date-fns` - for date calculations (YoY, QoQ analytics)
6. `npm install html2canvas` - for capturing chart visualizations in exports

**Acceptance:**
- All packages added to package.json dependencies
- Run `npm install` successfully
- No dependency conflicts
acceptance_criteria: recharts, xlsx, jspdf, jspdf-autotable, pptxgenjs, date-fns, html2canvas added to package.json
npm install completes without errors
No peer dependency warnings

tasks.title: Build analytics engine and ReportsContext
tasks.description:
Create the core analytics engine, data models, and state management for reporting.

**Implementation:**
1. Create `src/utils/analytics.js`:
   - `calculateYoYComparison(currentData, previousData)` - year-over-year % change
   - `calculateQoQComparison(currentData, previousData)` - quarter-over-quarter % change
   - `aggregateMetrics(projects, timeframe)` - aggregate project metrics by time period
   - `generateTrendData(projects, period)` - create time-series data for charts
   - Helper functions for date ranges, filtering, grouping

2. Create `src/types/reports.js` (or .ts):
   - Report template types (executive, detailed, risk, financial, performance, etc.)
   - Report configuration interface (filters, metrics, date ranges)
   - Export format types (PDF, Excel, PowerPoint)
   - Schedule configuration type

3. Create `src/context/ReportsContext.jsx`:
   - State: availableTemplates, customReports, schedules, exportQueue
   - Methods:
     - `generateReport(templateId, config)` - generate report from template
     - `createCustomReport(config)` - build custom report
     - `exportReport(reportId, format)` - export to PDF/Excel/PPT
     - `scheduleReport(reportId, schedule, stakeholders)` - set up automated distribution
     - `getReportData(reportId)` - fetch report data with analytics
   - Persist custom reports and schedules in localStorage or Firebase

4. Create `src/services/reportGenerator.js`:
   - Core logic for transforming project data into report format
   - Apply filters and date ranges
   - Calculate metrics and analytics
   - Format data for visualizations

**Testing:**
- Create `src/utils/analytics.test.js`
  - Test YoY calculation with sample data
  - Test QoQ calculation edge cases (missing quarters)
  - Test metric aggregation
  - Test trend data generation
- Create `src/context/ReportsContext.test.jsx`
  - Test report generation flow
  - Test custom report creation
  - Test schedule management
  - Mock Firebase/localStorage
- Create `src/services/reportGenerator.test.js`
  - Test data transformation
  - Test filtering logic
  - Test metric calculations
acceptance_criteria: Analytics utilities calculate YoY and QoQ comparisons correctly
ReportsContext manages report state and operations
Report data models and types defined
Report generator service transforms project data accurately
Comprehensive tests written and passing for all modules

tasks.title: Create report template library with 15+ templates
tasks.description:
Build the pre-configured report template library.

**Implementation:**
1. Create `src/config/reportTemplates.js`:
   Define 15+ report templates with configurations:
   - **Executive Summary** - high-level KPIs, project health, strategic alignment
   - **Detailed Analysis** - comprehensive project breakdown, timelines, resources
   - **Risk Report** - risk assessment, mitigation strategies, exposure analysis
   - **Financial Report** - budget vs actual, cost projections, ROI analysis
   - **Performance Dashboard** - velocity, completion rates, quality metrics
   - **Resource Utilization** - team allocation, capacity planning, workload
   - **Timeline Analysis** - schedule adherence, critical path, milestones
   - **Quality Metrics** - defect rates, test coverage, code quality
   - **Stakeholder Report** - progress updates, deliverables, next steps
   - **Portfolio Health** - overall portfolio status, trends, alerts
   - **Strategic Alignment** - business value, OKR progress, strategy fit
   - **Comparative Analysis** - project comparisons, benchmarking
   - **Trend Report** - historical trends, forecasts, seasonality
   - **Capacity Planning** - resource forecasts, bottleneck analysis
   - **ROI Analysis** - value realization, cost-benefit analysis

2. Each template configuration includes:
   - Template ID, name, description
   - Default metrics to display
   - Default visualizations (chart types)
   - Default time ranges
   - Suggested filters
   - Layout structure

3. Create `src/components/Reports/TemplateRenderer.jsx`:
   - Component that renders any template with provided data
   - Handles different template layouts
   - Renders metrics, charts, and tables based on template config

4. Create `src/components/Reports/TemplateSelector.jsx`:
   - UI to browse and select templates
   - Template preview cards with descriptions
   - Search and filter templates by category

**Testing:**
- Create `src/config/reportTemplates.test.js`
  - Validate all 15+ templates have required fields
  - Test template structure and configuration
- Create `src/components/Reports/TemplateRenderer.test.jsx`
  - Test rendering different template types
  - Test data binding
  - Mock report data
- Create `src/components/Reports/TemplateSelector.test.jsx`
  - Test template browsing
  - Test search/filter functionality
acceptance_criteria: At least 15 report templates defined with complete configurations
TemplateRenderer component renders any template correctly
TemplateSelector provides intuitive template browsing
All templates include appropriate metrics and visualizations
Tests written and passing for template system

tasks.title: Build report visualization components
tasks.description:
Create reusable visualization components for reports.

**Implementation:**
1. Create `src/components/Charts/` directory with chart components:
   - `LineChart.jsx` - for trend analysis (using recharts)
   - `BarChart.jsx` - for comparisons and distributions
   - `PieChart.jsx` - for proportions and breakdowns
   - `AreaChart.jsx` - for cumulative trends
   - `ComposedChart.jsx` - for multi-metric visualizations
   - Each chart should:
     - Accept data prop in standardized format
     - Support customizable colors, labels, axes
     - Be responsive and print-friendly
     - Show loading and error states

2. Create `src/components/Reports/MetricsCard.jsx`:
   - Display key metrics with YoY/QoQ comparison
   - Show percentage change with up/down indicators
   - Color-coded for positive/negative trends
   - Support different metric types (currency, percentage, count)

3. Create `src/components/Reports/DataTable.jsx`:
   - Sortable, filterable table for detailed data
   - Export table data to CSV
   - Pagination for large datasets
   - Customizable columns

4. Create `src/components/Reports/ReportPreview.jsx`:
   - Main component that combines all visualizations
   - Renders complete report with charts, tables, and metrics
   - Print-optimized layout
   - Export button integration

**Styling:**
- Create corresponding CSS files for each component
- Use consistent color scheme for charts
- Ensure components are responsive and print-friendly
- Follow existing component patterns in the project

**Testing:**
- Create test files for each component:
  - `src/components/Charts/LineChart.test.jsx`
  - `src/components/Charts/BarChart.test.jsx`
  - `src/components/Charts/PieChart.test.jsx`
  - `src/components/Reports/MetricsCard.test.jsx`
  - `src/components/Reports/DataTable.test.jsx`
  - `src/components/Reports/ReportPreview.test.jsx`
- Test rendering with sample data
- Test interactive features (sorting, filtering)
- Test responsive behavior
- Mock recharts components
acceptance_criteria: All chart components render correctly with sample data
MetricsCard displays YoY/QoQ comparisons accurately
DataTable supports sorting, filtering, and export
ReportPreview combines all components into cohesive report view
Components are responsive and print-friendly
Comprehensive tests written and passing

tasks.title: Implement custom report builder UI
tasks.description:
Build the custom report builder interface for creating ad-hoc reports.

**Implementation:**
1. Create `src/pages/ReportBuilder.jsx`:
   - Multi-step wizard for building custom reports:
     - Step 1: Select report type (or start from template)
     - Step 2: Configure filters (date range, projects, tags, status)
     - Step 3: Select metrics (choose from available KPIs)
     - Step 4: Choose visualizations (chart types for each metric)
     - Step 5: Configure layout and formatting
     - Step 6: Preview and save/export
   - Save custom reports for reuse
   - Name and describe custom reports

2. Create `src/components/ReportBuilder/FilterPanel.jsx`:
   - Date range picker (with presets: last 30 days, last quarter, last year, YTD)
   - Project multi-select dropdown
   - Status filter (active, completed, on hold)
   - Tag/category filter
   - Custom filter builder

3. Create `src/components/ReportBuilder/MetricsSelector.jsx`:
   - Checklist of available metrics
   - Grouped by category (financial, performance, quality, timeline)
   - Show metric descriptions on hover
   - Drag-and-drop to reorder selected metrics

4. Create `src/components/ReportBuilder/VisualizationPicker.jsx`:
   - Select chart type for each metric
   - Visual preview of chart types
   - Recommend appropriate chart types for each metric

5. Add route to `src/App.jsx`:
   - `/reports/builder` for the report builder page
   - `/reports/:id` for viewing saved reports

6. Create navigation link:
   - Add "Reports" to main navigation (Navbar)
   - Dropdown with "View Reports", "Build Custom Report", "Scheduled Reports"

**Testing:**
- Create `src/pages/ReportBuilder.test.jsx`
  - Test wizard navigation
  - Test report configuration
  - Test save functionality
- Create component tests:
  - `src/components/ReportBuilder/FilterPanel.test.jsx`
  - `src/components/ReportBuilder/MetricsSelector.test.jsx`
  - `src/components/ReportBuilder/VisualizationPicker.test.jsx`
- Test filter application
- Test metric selection and reordering
- Mock ReportsContext
acceptance_criteria: Report builder wizard guides users through custom report creation
Filters can be configured and applied correctly
Metrics can be selected and reordered
Visualizations can be chosen for each metric
Custom reports can be saved and loaded
Route added to App.jsx and navigation updated
Tests written and passing

tasks.title: Build multi-format export system (PDF, Excel, PowerPoint)
tasks.description:
Implement export functionality for PDF, Excel, and PowerPoint formats.

**Implementation:**
1. Create `src/services/exportService.js`:
   - `exportToPDF(reportData, config)` - generate PDF using jspdf
     - Include charts (convert to images with html2canvas)
     - Include tables with jspdf-autotable
     - Add headers, footers, page numbers
     - Company branding/logo
     - Table of contents for multi-page reports
   - `exportToExcel(reportData, config)` - generate Excel using xlsx
     - Multiple sheets for different report sections
     - Include raw data tables
     - Format cells (headers, currency, percentages)
     - Add charts if possible (Excel charts)
     - Include summary sheet with key metrics
   - `exportToPowerPoint(reportData, config)` - generate PPT using pptxgenjs
     - Title slide with report name and date
     - Slides for each major section
     - Include charts and key metrics
     - Speaker notes with additional context
     - Consistent template/theme

2. Create `src/components/Reports/ExportButton.jsx`:
   - Dropdown button with export format options
   - Show loading state during export generation
   - Download file automatically when ready
   - Handle errors gracefully
   - Show success message after download

3. Create `src/utils/chartToImage.js`:
   - Helper to convert chart components to images for PDF/PPT
   - Use html2canvas to capture rendered charts
   - Optimize image quality and size

4. Export configuration options:
   - Page size and orientation (PDF)
   - Include/exclude certain sections
   - Date format preferences
   - Company branding (logo, colors)

**Testing:**
- Create `src/services/exportService.test.js`
  - Test PDF generation with sample data
  - Test Excel generation and verify structure
  - Test PowerPoint generation
  - Verify file format validity (basic checks)
  - Test error handling for malformed data
- Create `src/components/Reports/ExportButton.test.jsx`
  - Test export button interaction
  - Test loading states
  - Test error handling
  - Mock exportService
- Create `src/utils/chartToImage.test.js`
  - Test chart capture
  - Mock html2canvas
acceptance_criteria: PDF export generates professional documents with charts and tables
Excel export creates multi-sheet workbooks with formatted data
PowerPoint export produces presentation-ready slides
Export button provides clear feedback during generation
All export formats handle errors gracefully
Comprehensive tests written and passing

tasks.title: Implement scheduling and distribution system
tasks.description:
Build automated report scheduling and distribution.

**Implementation:**
1. Create `src/pages/ReportSchedules.jsx`:
   - List of scheduled reports
   - Create new schedule button
   - Edit/delete existing schedules
   - View schedule history (past distributions)
   - Next scheduled run time display

2. Create `src/components/Reports/ScheduleForm.jsx`:
   - Select report to schedule (template or custom)
   - Configure frequency:
     - Daily, Weekly (select days), Monthly (select date), Quarterly, Yearly
     - Custom cron-like expression for advanced users
   - Start date and optional end date
   - Time of day to run
   - Export format for scheduled report (PDF, Excel, PPT)
   - Recipient configuration:
     - Email addresses (comma-separated)
     - Predefined stakeholder groups
     - Include report as attachment or download link

3. Create `src/services/schedulerService.js`:
   - `createSchedule(reportId, config)` - save schedule configuration
   - `getNextRunTime(schedule)` - calculate next execution time
   - `executeSchedule(scheduleId)` - generate and distribute report
   - Store schedules in Firebase or localStorage
   - Track execution history

4. Create `src/utils/stakeholderGroups.js`:
   - Manage stakeholder groups (e.g., "Executives", "Project Managers", "Team Leads")
   - CRUD operations for groups
   - Associate email addresses with groups

5. Distribution mechanism:
   - For MVP: generate download links and show notification
   - Future: integrate with email service (Firebase Functions, SendGrid)
   - Queue system for managing multiple scheduled reports

6. Add route to `src/App.jsx`:
   - `/reports/schedules` for schedule management
   - Update Reports navigation dropdown

**Testing:**
- Create `src/pages/ReportSchedules.test.jsx`
  - Test schedule list rendering
  - Test create/edit/delete operations
- Create `src/components/Reports/ScheduleForm.test.jsx`
  - Test form validation
  - Test frequency configuration
  - Test recipient management
- Create `src/services/schedulerService.test.js`
  - Test schedule creation and storage
  - Test next run time calculation
  - Test schedule execution logic
- Create `src/utils/stakeholderGroups.test.js`
  - Test group management CRUD
  - Test email association
acceptance_criteria: Users can create and manage report schedules
Frequency options support daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, yearly
Stakeholder groups can be defined and managed
Schedule execution generates and distributes reports
Next run time calculated correctly
Route added and navigation updated
Tests written and passing

tasks.title: Validate reporting and analytics features
tasks.description:
Run comprehensive test suite for the reporting and analytics system.

**Test files to validate:**
- src/utils/analytics.test.js
- src/context/ReportsContext.test.jsx
- src/services/reportGenerator.test.js
- src/config/reportTemplates.test.js
- src/components/Reports/TemplateRenderer.test.jsx
- src/components/Reports/TemplateSelector.test.jsx
- src/components/Charts/*.test.jsx (all chart tests)
- src/components/Reports/MetricsCard.test.jsx
- src/components/Reports/DataTable.test.jsx
- src/components/Reports/ReportPreview.test.jsx
- src/pages/ReportBuilder.test.jsx
- src/components/ReportBuilder/*.test.jsx (all builder component tests)
- src/services/exportService.test.js
- src/components/Reports/ExportButton.test.jsx
- src/utils/chartToImage.test.js
- src/pages/ReportSchedules.test.jsx
- src/components/Reports/ScheduleForm.test.jsx
- src/services/schedulerService.test.js
- src/utils/stakeholderGroups.test.js

**Validation steps:**
1. Run `npm test` to execute all tests
2. Verify all reporting and analytics tests pass
3. Check for TypeScript compilation errors
4. Verify no console errors or warnings
5. Check test coverage for reporting modules (target >80%)
6. Run lint checks: `npm run lint`

**Success criteria:**
- All tests pass
- No TypeScript errors
- Lint passes with no errors
- Test coverage meets minimum threshold
acceptance_criteria: All reporting tests pass
No TypeScript compilation errors
Lint check passes
Test coverage adequate (>80% for reporting modules)

tasks.title: Commit reporting and analytics feature implementation
tasks.description:
Create git commit for the complete reporting and analytics system.

**Files to commit:**
- package.json (new dependencies)
- src/utils/analytics.js and test
- src/types/reports.js
- src/context/ReportsContext.jsx and test
- src/services/reportGenerator.js and test
- src/config/reportTemplates.js and test
- src/components/Reports/ (all report components and tests)
- src/components/Charts/ (all chart components and tests)
- src/components/ReportBuilder/ (all builder components and tests)
- src/pages/ReportBuilder.jsx and test
- src/pages/ReportSchedules.jsx and test
- src/services/exportService.js and test
- src/services/schedulerService.js and test
- src/utils/chartToImage.js and test
- src/utils/stakeholderGroups.js and test
- src/App.jsx (modified for new routes)
- src/components/Navbar.jsx (modified for Reports navigation)
- Any other modified files

**Commit message:**
feat: Implement comprehensive portfolio reporting and analytics

- Add 15+ pre-configured report templates (executive, detailed, risk, financial, etc.)
- Build custom report builder with configurable filters, metrics, and visualizations
- Implement multi-format export (PDF, Excel, PowerPoint)
- Create automated report scheduling and distribution system
- Add year-over-year and quarter-over-quarter comparison analytics
- Include interactive charts and data visualizations using recharts
- Support stakeholder group management for report distribution

Closes EUN-76

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
acceptance_criteria: All reporting feature files committed
Commit message follows conventional commit format
References issue EUN-76
Includes co-author attribution
